steady_flame.name: The Steady Flame
steady_flame.tagline: You don't burn bright to be seen. You burn steady to keep others warm.
steady_flame.description: Your relationship is built on consistency. You show love through presence, through memory, through the way you just know. You're the person who remembers. Who notices. Who stays.
steady_flame.growth: Let yourself want more out loud. Steady doesn't mean silent.
steady_flame.triviaFact: Research shows that partners who have daily small rituals of connection — even just 6 seconds of eye contact — report 40% higher relationship satisfaction.

electric_spark.name: The Electric Spark
electric_spark.tagline: You make ordinary moments feel alive. That's not small — that's everything.
electric_spark.description: You're the energy. The one who books the spontaneous trip, who sends the voice note at midnight, who keeps the relationship from going quiet. You lead with action.
electric_spark.growth: Sometimes the deepest connection lives in stillness. Let a moment be, without filling it.
electric_spark.triviaFact: Novelty and shared new experiences activate the same neural pathways as early romantic love — which is why spontaneity keeps long-term relationships feeling alive.

deep_current.name: The Deep Current
deep_current.tagline: You feel everything. You just don't always say it. That's the work.
deep_current.description: You love with enormous depth. You think carefully, feel intensely, and care about the truth of things. You can read a room before anyone else. You just need to let people read you back.
deep_current.growth: Your partner can't know what they can't see. Give them a draft, not the finished version.
deep_current.triviaFact: People who score high on emotional intelligence report stronger relationship quality — but only when they share that intelligence outward, not just use it to observe.

shifting_tide.name: The Shifting Tide
shifting_tide.tagline: You're honest enough to name what others avoid. That's rare. Use it gently.
shifting_tide.description: You adapt. You grow. You're willing to have the uncomfortable conversation others keep postponing. Relationships with you don't go stale — they evolve. You create movement.
shifting_tide.growth: Not every truth needs to land right away. Timing is its own form of caring.
shifting_tide.triviaFact: Couples who regularly discuss dissatisfactions — rather than avoid them — report higher long-term satisfaction, even when those conversations are initially difficult.
